runs.assessment: Portfolio holds only 2 positions (AAPL and TSLA) entered on 2026-04-28, with critical data gaps — all fundamental and momentum metrics are zeroed out (eps_growth=0, momentum_rank=0, ma_50d=0), making rule-based sell decisions data-dependent. Cash is severely over-allocated at 59.1% against a bull regime target of 5%, and the full rebalance scheduled for 2026-05-01 (Thursday) is the priority action. AAPL has an earnings event on 2026-04-30, and market breadth at 57% is narrowing — warranting a cautious but not bearish stance.
flags: TSLA: Position weight at 38.2% dramatically exceeds the 8% maximum position size cap — must be corrected at 2026-05-01 rebalance; this is the highest urgency structural risk in the portfolio
TSLA: 30-day annualized volatility of 44.55% almost certainly places it above the 90th percentile of the S&P 500 universe — Risk Filter (Rule 4) likely triggered; verify at rebalance screening

TSLA: EPS growth reported as 0% for this entry — if confirmed as a second consecutive quarter below 5%, Rule 3 (quality deterioration) will trigger a mandatory sell; this is the first confirmed quarter, second quarter watch active
TSLA: Forward P/E data currently 0 (unavailable) — prior run flagged 82.1x which fails the absolute valuation guard rail; must re-verify at next screen
AAPL: Earnings on 2026-04-30 (2 trading days away) — flag as WATCH with earnings risk; do not sell solely for earnings but note elevated event risk heading into Thursday rebalance
AAPL: Revenue growth previously flagged at 4%, below the 8% quality threshold — only passing 1 of 2 quality criteria; EPS growth also shows 0 in current data (data gap likely) — monitor at rebalance

PORTFOLIO STRUCTURE: 59.1% cash vs 5% bull-regime target — portfolio is critically under-deployed; full rebalance on 2026-05-01 must add 13-18 new positions to reach 15-20 holding target

decisions.reason: Earnings on 2026-04-30 (BMO or TAS) creates overnight gap risk 2 days out. Momentum rank, MA50, and fundamental data fields are all zero — cannot confirm or deny sell rules from current data. Prior flags: revenue growth 4% below 8% threshold (1 of 2 quality criteria failing). Do not sell ahead of earnings without a confirmed rule trigger, but flag for immediate review at rebalance. If earnings miss occurs, re-check Rule 3 quality deterioration trigger.

Multiple structural concerns persist: (1) Weight at 38.2% — 4.8x the 8% maximum, must be reduced at rebalance; (2) Volatility at 44.55% annualized almost certainly exceeds the 90th percentile Risk Filter threshold — likely failing Rule 4 risk screen; (3) EPS growth 0% is first flagged quarter below 5% — one more quarter triggers Rule 3 mandatory sell; (4) All momentum and MA data are zero (data gap) so Rule 1 and Rule 2 cannot be confirmed today. No parabolic blow-off (PnL only +0.72% in 0 days). Hold until rebalance but treat as highest priority for sizing correction and potential exit if screening confirms filter failures.

runs.cash_rationale: Cash at 59.1% is grossly misaligned with the bull regime target of 5%. The 2026-05-01 monthly rebalance is the correct mechanism to deploy this capital into 13-18 new momentum-qualified positions. No intra-day buys are warranted today given the AAPL earnings risk on Thursday and the fact that the full screening process should be run at rebalance. Cash should decrease to approximately 5% of portfolio at the rebalance by adding new qualifying positions.
runs.summary: Day-end review: HOLD both positions with WATCH flags — data gaps prevent definitive rule triggers today, AAPL earnings on Thu Apr 30 noted, TSLA concentration and volatility remain critical risks to resolve at Thursday's full rebalance.

runs.assessment: Portfolio is in early-stage deployment with only 2 of a target 15–20 positions filled, leaving cash at 59.1% versus the bull-regime target of 5%. TSLA dominates at 38.1% weight (well above the 8% max position size cap) while AAPL is severely underweight at 2.7%. Market regime is confirmed bull with VIX at 18.2, positive 200-day MA trend, and 63% breadth — all three signals aligned.
flags: TSLA: Position weight at 38.1% dramatically exceeds the 8% maximum position size rule — highest urgency concentration risk requiring correction at next rebalance on 2026-05-01

TSLA: Forward P/E of 82.1 fails the valuation guard rail (Fwd P/E < 40); passes only if confirmed within top 70% cheapest by sector (Consumer Discretionary) — valuation data should be verified before next rebalance
TSLA: EPS growth reported as 0% — if this persists for a second consecutive quarter it will trigger quality deterioration sell rule (Rule 3: EPS growth < 5% x2 quarters); monitor closely
AAPL: Revenue growth of 4% is below the 8% quality filter threshold — currently passing 1 of 2 quality criteria only; watch for further deterioration
PORTFOLIO: Cash at 59.1% is drastically above the 5% bull-regime target — the portfolio is significantly under-deployed and not aligned with the current bull allocation mandate
PORTFOLIO: Only 2 positions held versus target of 15–20 — portfolio is structurally incomplete and under-diversified, amplifying single-name risk on TSLA

decisions.reason: Momentum rank #1, price well above 50-day MA ($376.30 vs MA50 $248.50), PnL +0.52% well below profit-take threshold of +40%. No sell rule triggered. However, forward P/E of 82.1 is a valuation flag and EPS growth of 0% is approaching quality deterioration trigger. Hold for now but flag for rebalance size correction.

Momentum rank #2, price marginally below cost ($271.06 vs avg cost $272.61, -0.57%) but well above 50-day MA ($271.06 vs MA50 $215.30). Revenue growth of 4% fails quality filter threshold of 8% — single criterion failure. No sell rule technically triggered yet. Monitor revenue trend; if next data refresh still shows <8% revenue growth this should be escalated at rebalance.

runs.cash_rationale: Cash at 59.1% is far above the bull-regime target of 5%. The portfolio needs significant additional capital deployment across 13–18 new positions to align with strategy mandates. This should be addressed at the upcoming monthly rebalance on 2026-05-01. No trades should be placed pre-market today (Day Start run — flag only), but the monthly rebalance in 5 days must prioritize aggressive cash deployment into screened momentum candidates.
runs.summary: Pre-market check: bull regime confirmed (VIX 18.2, breadth 63%), no immediate sell rules triggered, but portfolio is critically under-deployed at 59% cash with TSLA overweight at 38% — full rebalance urgently needed on 2026-05-01.

runs.assessment: Portfolio is early-stage with only 2 holdings (TSLA and AAPL) and a critically underdeployed equity position at 40.86% invested versus the 95% bull-regime target — cash sits at 59.14% against a 5% target. Both positions are technically healthy (above 50-day MA, strong momentum ranks), but TSLA raises quality and valuation concerns with 0% EPS growth and a forward P/E of 82.1, and AAPL shows revenue growth of only 4% which fails the quality filter threshold of 8%. Market regime is confirmed bull (VIX 18.2, positive MA200 trend, breadth 63%) with 0.78 confidence.
flags: TSLA: EPS growth reported as 0% — fails quality filter (requires >10%); forward P/E of 82.1 also fails the absolute valuation guard rail (<40); confirm whether sector-relative valuation (top 70% by Consumer Discretionary) rescues this position before next rebalance
TSLA: Despite 0% EPS growth, revenue growth of 13% is solid — quality filter is split-failing; one more quarter of sub-5% EPS growth would trigger Rule 3 (quality deterioration) sell

AAPL: Revenue growth of 4% fails the quality filter threshold of 8% — position is currently passing momentum and valuation screens but failing quality; this is a borderline hold that should be reviewed at monthly rebalance on 2026-05-01

PORTFOLIO: Equity exposure is 40.86% vs. bull-regime target of 95% — the portfolio is severely underdeployed; next rebalance on 2026-05-01 should screen and add 13-18 additional positions to reach target of 15-20 holdings at correct weights
AAPL: Position weight is 2.7% — below the minimum position size floor of 5%; this is a structural issue from insufficient capital allocation, not a sell signal, but flags the need for rebalancing
TSLA: Position weight is 38.1% — well above the 8% maximum position size cap; this represents extreme concentration risk and must be addressed at next rebalance

decisions.reason: Price is $376.30 vs 50-day MA of $248.50 — well above trend line, no trend break. Momentum rank #1 in portfolio. However, 0% EPS growth is a quality concern and forward P/E of 82.1 exceeds the absolute <40 threshold. No sell rules triggered today but quality deterioration rule (Rule 3) is at risk if EPS growth remains below 5% for a second consecutive quarter. Additionally, position weight of 38.1% far exceeds the 8% max — structural overweight to be corrected at rebalance.

Price is $271.06 vs 50-day MA of $215.30 — comfortably above trend, no trend break. Momentum rank #2. EPS growth of 12% passes quality filter but revenue growth of 4% fails the 8% threshold — position is technically failing the quality filter on one of two criteria. Forward P/E of 26.2 is well within valuation guard rail. No immediate sell rule triggered but quality filter failure warrants close monitoring into next rebalance. Position weight of 2.7% is below the 5% minimum floor.

runs.cash_rationale: Cash is at 59.14% versus the bull-regime target of 5%. The portfolio is massively underdeployed relative to strategy parameters. While no action is taken pre-market today, the monthly rebalance on 2026-05-01 must aggressively deploy capital into 13-18 new qualifying positions. The current cash level is not a deliberate defensive posture — it reflects an incomplete portfolio build. No intraday cash action warranted today (pre-market, no trades placed), but cash reduction is the clear directive for the upcoming rebalance.
runs.summary: Pre-market check complete — no sell rules triggered on TSLA or AAPL, but both positions carry filter concerns (TSLA quality/valuation, AAPL revenue growth), and portfolio is critically underdeployed at 41% equity vs. 95% bull-regime target ahead of the May 1 rebalance.
